method: Yarrow Stem (蓍草 shīcǎo) Probability Weights
description: The traditional yarrow stem method of I Ching divination produces four possible line values per cast. These arise from dividing 49 stalks into groups three times, counting remainders. The probabilities are exact fractions.

line_values.name: Old Yin (太陰 Tài Yīn)
line_values.description: Moving yin line — broken, but in motion. Changes to yang in the resultant hexagram. Rarest line type. Represents fulfilled yin energy ready to transform.

line_values.name: Young Yang (少陽 Shào Yáng)
line_values.description: Stable yang line — solid, at rest. The line you want most often. Does not change. Second most common.

line_values.name: Young Yin (少陰 Shào Yīn)
line_values.description: Stable yin line — broken, at rest. The most common line. Does not change. Represents quiescent receptivity.

line_values.name: Old Yang (太陽 Tài Yáng)
line_values.description: Moving yang line — solid, but in motion. Changes to yin in the resultant hexagram. Third most common. Represents creative force at its peak, about to transform.

probability_derivation.note: Three divisions of 49 stalks. Each division assigns stalks to piles and counts by groups of 4. The remainders (1 or 2 for odd divisions, 2 or 4 for even) determine line value.
probability_derivation.operations: Divide 49 stalks by hand 3 times per line, count remainder groups mod 4
probability_derivation.glsl_cast_function: float castLine(float seed) { float r = hash(seed); if (r < 1.0/16.0) return 6.0; if (r < 6.0/16.0) return 7.0; if (r < 13.0/16.0) return 8.0; return 9.0; }

changing_line_transformation.note: When a hexagram has changing lines, it transforms to a second hexagram. Moving lines flip; stable lines stay.
changing_line_transformation.rule_6_to_yang: Old yin (6) → young yang (7): broken line becomes solid
changing_line_transformation.rule_9_to_yin: Old yang (9) → young yin (8): solid line becomes broken
changing_line_transformation.rule_stable: Young yang (7) and young yin (8) remain unchanged

coin_method_comparison.description: The three-coin method gives different probabilities from yarrow stems:

coin_probabilities.7_young_yang: 6/16 = 0.375 (2 tails 1 head: value 7 = tail=2, head=3, sum=7)

coin_method_comparison.note: Yarrow stem method gives more young yin (stable) lines, fewer old yin (moving yin). The oracle is more stable under yarrow stems.
